sourceMovie.title: Taxi Driver
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Taxi Driver works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Collateral
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of Taxi Driver is alive in Collateral (2004), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Once Upon a Time in America
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Once Upon a Time in America (1984) operates in the same crime as Taxi Driver, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: American Gangster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: American Gangster (2007) belongs to the same conversation as Taxi Driver — both anchor their crime in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Blow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Taxi Driver's crime stayed with you, Blow (2001) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Bone Collector
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Taxi Driver excels in crime, The Bone Collector (1999) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Carlito's Way
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Carlito's Way (1993) is the natural next step after Taxi Driver: same crime DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: 25th Hour
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Taxi Driver who respond to its crime will find 25th Hour (2002) immediately familiar. Both films share a drama sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Serpico
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Taxi Driver fans keep landing on Serpico (1973) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Payback
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Payback draws from the same crime well as Taxi Driver, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1999 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Infiltrator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Infiltrator (2016) captures the crime appeal of Taxi Driver without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts The Infiltrator at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

faq.question: What should I watch after Taxi Driver?
faq.answer: Try Collateral, Once Upon a Time in America, American Gangster—each shares DNA with Taxi Driver while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Taxi Driver?
faq.answer: Taxi Driver (1976) is best described as Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Taxi Driver worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
